Jhelum. Roughly speaking, it comprised the districts of Jhelum,
Shahpur and Gujarat.
Vascitis are the Ossadioi of Alexander's historians, settled in
the region between the Indus and Jhelum, comprising Rawalpindi.
Vasati is mentioned in the Mahftbh&sya (IV. 2.52) and the MahamS-
yuri.
The Arjunayanas are identified by Fleet with the Kalacuris
who trace their descent from Kartavirya Arjuna, But according to
the Kasika (IT. 4.66) they were discended from the Pandava hero
Arjuna. The find-spots of their coins indicate that their country,
known as Arjunayanaka, lay within the triangle of Delhi-Jaipur-
Agra.
Adar'sa is the place where the Sarasvati disappears. So the
word, meaning the country or its people, is derived from Adarsa. It
was regarded as the western boundary of Aryavarta. According to
another view, it lay not far from the ancient kingdoms of Srughna
